output:
**The answer is (B) 35–40 minutes.**

The math from the stated parameters:

- 40% capacity increase → 20–40% increase in VMT (applying the 5–10% per 10% rule)
- That alone claws back roughly half to all of the capacity gain
- The 15% transit ridership drop adds *more* cars to the road, further eroding the benefit

But "fully negates" (C) overshoots what the numbers support. A 40% capacity increase with 20–40% induced demand still leaves some net capacity gain — just a diminished one. The transit effect is real but not enough to push commute times back to 45+ within 3 years.

So you get the classic induced demand outcome: a real but partial improvement, landing around **35–40 minutes**.
